Your reply dated March 14, 1978 to my letter dated January 21, 
1978 left unanswered one aspect which I may not have made clear. 
Let us take the case of a major donor who in 1977 made and re-
ported to the City of Los Angeles a contribution to a testi-
monial dinner for a city official. 
During the first half of 
calendar year 1978 the major donor makes contributions to com-
mittees supporting state wide propositions and duly reports its 
contributions with the Secretary of State, the Registar of 
Voters in San Francisco and the Registrar/Recorder of Los Angeles 
County. 
Is it also necessary to report those state wide contri-
butions to the City of Los Angeles on a semi-annual campaign 
statement, bearing in mind that 1978 is an off-year for the City 
of Los Angeles? 
To change the above facts a little, assume that the major donor 
in the first half of 1978 contributes to a testimonial dinner 
for a Los Angeles City official. 
In such a case the contributions 
would be duly reported to the city on a semi-annual campaign 
statement since 1978 is an off-year for the city_ 
Is it necessary 
on such campaign statement also to report contributions made to 
committees on state wide propositions? 
Your auvice in regard to the foregoing will be greatly appreciated. 
Very truly yours, 
BP:eh

sections.question: Your reply dated March 14, 1978 to my letter dated January 21, 1978 left unanswered one aspect which I may not have made clear. Let us take the case of a major donor who in 1977 made and reported to the City of Los Angeles a contribution to a testimonial dinner for a city official. During the first half of calendar year 1978 the major donor makes contributions to committees supporting state wide propositions and duly reports its contributions with the Secretary of State, the Registar of Voters in San Francisco and the Registrar/Recorder of Los Angeles County. Is it also necessary to report those state wide contributions to the City of Los Angeles on a semi-annual campaign statement, bearing in mind that 1978 is an off-year for the City of Los Angeles? To change the above facts a little, assume that the major donor in the first half of 1978 contributes to a testimonial dinner for a Los Angeles City official. In such a case the contributions would be duly reported to the city on a semi-annual campaign statement since 1978 is an off-year for the city. Is it necessary on such campaign statement also to report contributions made to committees on state wide propositions?
sections.conclusion: Under your first set of facts a major donor who made a contribution to a Los Angeles City councilman in 1977 and then engages in political activity outside Los Angeles in early 1978 is required to file with the state for the primary election but owes the City of Los Angeles nothing with respect to 1978 state activity, as major donors do not carry over cumulative activity from one calendar year to the next. Under your second set of facts where the major donor makes both a city contribution and state contributions in early 1978, the donor is required to file with the state for the June 6, 1978 election and shall file copies of state reports with the Los Angeles city filing officer at the time those statements are due for the state election; no separate semi-annual statements are required for the city during that period.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a major donor must report state-level contributions to the City of Los Angeles when the donor also makes local contributions, particularly in off-election years for the city. The inquiry addresses two scenarios: contributions to state propositions with prior local activity, and concurrent local and state contributions in 1978.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A major donor's reporting obligations depend on whether activities are carried forward between calendar years and the timing of elections. When state and local activities occur in different calendar years, they are reported separately to each jurisdiction; when both occur in the same period, state reports should be copied to the local jurisdiction without duplicative semi-annual filings.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a follow-up inquiry with attached FPPC response. The conclusion section is fragmented and contains OCR errors ("tJnder", "ampaigna", "o:lntributiona"), making some phrases difficult to parse with complete certainty. The question is clear and extracted verbatim from the incoming request letter dated May 22, 1978. The response letter from FPPC is dated June 13, 1978 and signed by D. Giansello, Political Advisor.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Your reply dated March 14, 1978 to my letter dated January 21, 1978 left unanswered one aspect which I may not have made clear. Let us take the case of a major donor who in 1977 made and reported to the City of Los Angeles a contribution to a testimonial dinner for a city official. During the first half of calendar year 1978 the major donor makes contributions to committees supporting state wide propositions and duly reports its contributions with the Secretary of State, the Registar of Voters in San Francisco and the Registrar/Recorder of Los Angeles County. Is it also necessary to report those state wide contributions to the City of Los Angeles on a semi-annual campaign statement, bearing in mind that 1978 is an off-year for the City of Los Angeles? To change the above facts a little, assume that the major donor in the first half of 1978 contributes to a testimonial dinner for a Los Angeles City official. In such a case the contributions would be duly reported to the city on a semi-annual campaign statement since 1978 is an off-year for the city. Is it necessary on such campaign statement also to report contributions made to committees on state wide propositions?

CONCLUSION: Under your first set of facts a major donor who made a contribution to a Los Angeles City councilman in 1977 and then engages in political activity outside Los Angeles in early 1978 is required to file with the state for the primary election but owes the City of Los Angeles nothing with respect to 1978 state activity, as major donors do not carry over cumulative activity from one calendar year to the next. Under your second set of facts where the major donor makes both a city contribution and state contributions in early 1978, the donor is required to file with the state for the June 6, 1978 election and shall file copies of state reports with the Los Angeles city filing officer at the time those statements are due for the state election; no separate semi-annual statements are required for the city during that period.

embedding.summary: This advice letter addresses the reporting requirements for a major donor making contributions at both state and local levels, clarifying whether state-level contributions must be reported to local jurisdictions and how cumulative activity is calculated across calendar years and election cycles.
